results.possibleIdentity: William F. Gilmore was a technical professional and physicist associated with International Business Machines (IBM) in the late 1960s and early 1970s. Based in Vestal and Endicott, New York, he specialized in technical fields including physics, optics, lasers, and holography. Gilmore was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) Engineering Registry, a service organization founded by artists Robert Rauschenberg and Robert Whitman alongside engineers Billy Klüver and Fred Waldhauer to foster collaborations between the arts and scientific communities. During this period, the IBM facility in Endicott was a significant hub for optical research, specifically for the development of holographic systems and laser-based technology such as early barcode scanning prototypes.
